svg.font-family: Segoe UI, Helvetica Neue, Arial, sans-serif
svg.text: THOR vs mimir - the honest picture
Same machine, blind-judged, re-measured fresh in the V5 round + V6/V7 code re-judges (3-judge median, blind maps).
deliberate recall over the live store . higher is better
Test 1 overall THOR 63.8% vs mimir 64.0% (n=200) - a statistical tie
Clean split: THOR leads every knowledge category (decision, gotcha, docs, config); mimir leads both code categories.
Jury strictness moved the absolutes asymmetrically vs the previous round (THOR 68.5 -> 63.8, mimir 59.8 -> 64.0), same corpus.
TEST 1 ADDENDUM - CODE RE-JUDGED (V6 + V7 rounds, same 93 items)
serving parity + path affinity, then the derived symbol sidecar . fresh blind mini-juries
Behavior flipped and held above 70 twice (60.0 -> 71.7 -> 72.5 vs mimir 62.5 -> 57.5 -> 59.2). Structure went
42.4 -> 50.0 -> 54.5 while mimir fell 63.6 -> 57.6 -> 56.1: a 21-point gap closed to 1.6 - and excluding four
dead-source items (files deliberately removed; both systems score zero) both judge at exactly 60.3% vs 60.3%.
New agent tools from the same round: where_used / impact / outline on a derived, rebuildable symbol sidecar.
TEST 2 - SAME KNOWLEDGE (facts both systems verifiably hold)
RE-MEASURED on a rebuilt shared subset . cuts over the judged Test 1 medians
THOR takes the strict dual-written cut FOR THE FIRST TIME (96.2% vs 93.4%) - pure memory recall was mimir's home
turf across four earlier juries. mimir takes the broad cut, which is two-thirds code/doc-chunk questions.
TEST 3 - MULTI-PROJECT (three private project repos seeded)
45 real questions from each repo, scoped per project, blind-judged by 3-judge median . top-5 full chunks
Test 3 overall THOR 92.2% vs mimir 92.2% (n=45) - a dead tie
mimir's previous outright lead (98.9% vs 92.2%) is gone. Per-project numbers on n=15 move with a single question
(6.7 points each): treat the project split as noisy and the overall tie as the takeaway.
post-compaction, empty context: does the AS-DEPLOYED auto-injection surface the fact that stops the drift?
THOR's best drift numbers to date, judged THREE-WAY BLIND for the first time (courier / deliberate / mimir shuffled
onto anonymous sides in one jury pass). The as-deployed courier now also beats THOR's own deliberate recall
(62.5 / 48.6) - author-declared bilingual fires-when triggers fire on task prompts score-only ranking misses.
Pins + file/command guards cover the rest by construction: cargo run --example drift_eval, reproducible in-repo.
SPEED AND TOKENS (per-prompt cost, lower is better)
THOR cold vs mimir's as-deployed cold path, median of 20, canonical fixed 20-prompt set
NEW: THOR's own opt-in warm inject daemon (idea credit mimir) serves the provably IDENTICAL decision at 192.7 ms.
mimir's opt-in warm daemon stays the fastest channel outright - 38.9 ms, ~32 tokens - but served NOTHING on 10 of
the 20 prompts (single floor-gated memory): faster and cheaper because it serves less. THOR's cold median is back
under its own 250 ms guardrail (253 -> 206.8 ms). Old '1.5x faster / 2.1x fewer tokens' headline stays retired.
where THOR does not win, and the caveats
Test 1 overall is a tie now, not a THOR lead - and the movement was asymmetric (THOR down, mimir up, same corpus)
Code-structure: level but not won (54.5 vs 56.1 re-judged; 60.3-60.3 excl. dead sources) - 70% target unmet
mimir wins the broad shared cut (81.2% vs 77.0%) - two-thirds code questions, same weakness on an equal corpus
The 80%-everywhere goal still does not stand: 0 of 8 v4 gates (config how-to closest at 79.4%, drift surfaced 72.1%)
mimir's warm daemon stays fastest outright (38.9 vs 192.7 ms warm); THOR injects ~3.3x more tokens than mimir cold
Three drift golds stay honest misses (deep drift, zero shared vocabulary) - left as gaps, not trigger-stuffed
mimir has a code-symbol graph (graph/outline/peek) that THOR deliberately does NOT - for 'which functions call X'
Semantic mode needs a ~235 MB model + a warm embed-daemon resident (off by default; degrades cleanly to bm25)
Newer and far less battle-tested than mimir's daily use
One machine, private corpus, LLM-judged - only drift is repo-reproducible (examples/drift_eval.rs)
Bottom line coverage tie (63.8 vs 64.0) . strict dual-written 96.2 vs 93.4, 1st win . multi-project tie
drift 72 vs 59 . code re-judge: behavior 72.5 vs 59.2, structure level . 2.8x faster than mimir cold
